script:
Up.
Feet on the floor.
Shorts on. Now.

David Goggins ran 100 miles on two stress-fractured feet.
He didn't negotiate with the bed.
He didn't check his phone.
He put his shoes on in the dark and moved.

That's not inspiration. That's just what the body does when the mind has already decided.

You already decided.
Last night, you said it out loud:
"Do the morning lift before opening any work doc."

That decision is done. It's not up for review.
The only question left is mechanical.

No one is coming. It's on you.

Shorts are on.
Water bottle in hand.
Front door.
Walk to the gym.

Not the whole workout.
Not the whole day.
Just the door.
Open it.
Step through.
The bar is already loaded with your name.

{
  "script": "Up.
Feet on the floor.
Shorts. Now. Water bottle in hand.

David Goggins ran 100 miles on two broken feet.
Not metaphorically.
Actually broken.
He taped them and kept moving.
He was not inspired.
He was decided.

You decided last night.
You said it out loud:
"Do the morning lift before opening any work doc."
That is still true.
Nothing changed while you slept.

No one is coming. It's on you.

The gym door is the only decision left.
Not the workout.
Not the weight on the bar.
Just the door.

Shorts are on.
Walk.",

  "word_count": 107
}
